jokes.text: I can literally make anything sound positive. Your house just burned down, and you lost all your money in the stock market. It's a chance to start over. Fire is cleansing, and true wealth is measured by the amount of love in your life.
jokes.setup: Chris claims he can make anything sound positive
jokes.punchline: His relentlessly upbeat spin on complete disasters
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - only Chris could deliver this with such earnest conviction. The escalation from house fire to stock market loss with increasingly absurd positive spin is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: If I had to have anybody tell me that I had cancer, I would want it to be me.
jokes.setup: Chris continuing his positive spin demonstration
jokes.punchline: The dark implication that he'd want to deliver his own cancer diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark escalation that's perfectly in character. The economy is excellent - maximum impact with minimal words.

jokes.text: Ooh, I can't believe our house is going to be full of rich people named Dottie and Todd and Rick.
jokes.punchline: His specific, oddly accurate naming of rich people
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy observation - the specificity of the names feels authentic to his character's odd insights.

jokes.text: And I will be providing my world-famous $100 lap dances.
jokes.setup: Ron mentions providing ribs for the party
jokes.punchline: April's completely inappropriate party contribution
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan delivery of something completely inappropriate for a political donor event.

jokes.text: Sweet! / No.
jokes.punchline: Andy's enthusiasm immediately shut down by Ben
jokes.explanation: Quick reaction beat that perfectly captures Andy's naivety and Ben's exasperation.

jokes.text: 4 feet. Exact same size as the podium you use in the Parks department.
jokes.setup: Leslie commenting on the debate venue size
jokes.punchline: Ben's obsessively specific measurement knowledge
jokes.explanation: Classic Ben detail-obsessed character moment - the specificity feels authentic to his accountant background.

jokes.text: You could debate Newport in your sleep. / I have. / I know. We sleep in the same bed. It's been hell.
jokes.setup: Ben's confidence in Leslie's debate skills
jokes.punchline: The revelation that she literally debates in her sleep
jokes.explanation: Excellent three-beat structure with perfect escalation. The final 'it's been hell' lands perfectly as both relationship humor and character insight.

jokes.text: You're going to rip out his spine with your teeth and then chew it up and gargle with it.
jokes.punchline: His comically violent metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good escalation humor showing Ben's attempt to match Leslie's intensity, though slightly over-written.

jokes.text: I love it when you're needlessly disgusting.
jokes.punchline: Leslie's affectionate reaction to his gross imagery
jokes.explanation: Sweet relationship moment that doubles as character humor - Leslie finds Ben's attempts at aggression endearing.

jokes.text: Leave it messy. It doesn't matter. Nothing matters. Life is garbage.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to clean for the party
jokes.punchline: Tom's spiral from practical advice to existential despair
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from mundane to melodramatic - the progression feels natural for Tom's dramatic personality.

jokes.text: So we're up in the club. It's me! We're dancing. We're sweating. Let's just say we got to second that night. Kind of. Me so horny.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his radio shout-out to Ann
jokes.punchline: His cringeworthy oversharing on radio
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom cringe - perfectly captures his poor judgment and inappropriate oversharing.

jokes.text: I guess she's just afraid of how powerful her feelings are.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining why Ann broke up with him
jokes.punchline: His delusional interpretation of the obvious reason
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom delusion - he completely misreads the situation in a way that's totally in character.

jokes.text: Yes, I do, I care about... Andy... And champion.
jokes.setup: Andy saying April doesn't care about things
jokes.punchline: Her minimal list with her dog getting equal weight to Andy
jokes.explanation: Classic April - the pause before admitting she cares, and putting her dog on equal footing with Andy.

jokes.text: Leslie Knope is literally overflowing with ideas for this town. And speaking of methane, have you heard about her plan to limit greenhouse gas emissions?
jokes.setup: Asked to spin Leslie farting and throwing up
jokes.punchline: His seamless transition from bodily functions to environmental policy
jokes.explanation: Masterful spin work that's perfectly Chris - the methane connection is brilliant and shows real comedic craft.

jokes.text: I've heard that you and Tom Haverford are no longer romantically involved. Is that true? / Yes, we broke up. / And I honestly can't believe we ever dated. / Does defy logic.
jokes.setup: Chris asking about Ann's relationship status
jokes.punchline: His agreement that their relationship defied logic
jokes.explanation: Nice beat where Chris accidentally agrees that Tom and Ann made no sense together while trying to ask her out.

jokes.text: Okay, I still have feelings for you. Strong feelings. Emotional, primal feelings.
jokes.punchline: His intensely dramatic description of basic romantic feelings
jokes.explanation: Classic Chris over-intensity. The escalation to 'primal feelings' is funny but not particularly surprising for his character.

jokes.text: Maybe not for long. If Newport wins, the city council may replace me. But it would open it up for us to be together.
jokes.setup: Ann mentioning his workplace rule against dating
jokes.punchline: Chris suggesting his potential firing as a dating opportunity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chris's ability to find the positive angle even in potential job loss.

jokes.text: I recently invested in some shirts I got at a garage sale. Left those at Wendy's on the way home.
jokes.punchline: Andy's misunderstanding of investment and losing his 'investment' immediately
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - the idea that buying garage sale shirts is investing, then immediately losing them.

jokes.text: Hello. You are here because you gave us money. Now we will give you ribs. Also you will watch the debate. If you like the debate, you'll give us more money. That is all. Ron Swanson.
jokes.punchline: His brutally direct transactional explanation
jokes.explanation: Peak Ron Swanson - his complete disregard for political niceties and stating the obvious transaction.

jokes.text: Uh-oh. Somebody forgot to pay the cable bill. Am I right? / It was me.
jokes.setup: Andy making a joke about cable being out
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact admission of responsibility
jokes.explanation: Good relationship beat showing April's indifference to household responsibilities.

jokes.text: Nothing gets me more amped than Sarah McLachlan.
jokes.setup: Leslie pumping herself up for the debate
jokes.punchline: Her choice of the least amping music possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie contradiction - her earnest enthusiasm for the most mellowing music possible.

jokes.text: Here's what I know. I love you, and I like you.
jokes.punchline: Her Parks and Rec callback phrasing
jokes.explanation: Beautiful callback to Leslie's mother's advice - economical, character-perfect, and emotionally resonant.

jokes.text: Expectations are crazy low. If he puts two sentences together without crying, the press is going to say he's doing surprisingly well, and if he falls to pieces, he's going to look sympathetic. It's a win-win.
jokes.punchline: The cynical but accurate political calculation
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire showing how low expectations can be advantageous - rings very true.

jokes.text: And I am legendary newswoman Joan Callamezzo, newly single.
jokes.setup: Joan introducing herself as debate moderator
jokes.punchline: Her inappropriate personal disclosure
jokes.explanation: Classic Joan unprofessionalism - making the debate about herself immediately.

jokes.text: I want to tell you about my idea for assault rifle vending machines.
jokes.setup: Fester introducing himself as gun store owner
jokes.punchline: His completely unhinged policy proposal
jokes.explanation: Effective absurdist escalation from gun store owner to completely insane policy proposal.

jokes.text: Well, I've produced and starred in over 400 adult films this year alone.
jokes.setup: Brandi asking what an adult film star knows about politics
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact sharing of an impossible production schedule
jokes.explanation: The math joke of 400+ films in one year works but feels like low-hanging fruit.

jokes.text: And just like Leslie, I know what it's like to be the only woman in a room full of men.
jokes.punchline: Her comparison to Leslie with very different context
jokes.explanation: Smart double meaning that works on multiple levels - technically true but with very different implications.

jokes.text: That--that hurt my feelings. Hurt my feelings. You're supposed to be this positive person. Can't we just talk about things we like?
jokes.punchline: His childlike response to political criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bobby Newport - treating political debate like a hurt friend, completely missing the point.

jokes.text: Ah, that's okay, Joan. / Looking at you, Leslie.
jokes.setup: Joan telling candidates to keep it civil
jokes.punchline: Bobby's oblivious agreement followed by Joan's bias
jokes.explanation: Good beat showing Bobby's obliviousness and Joan's unprofessional bias against Leslie.

jokes.text: I want your vote because I want Pawnee and my dad to see what I'm made of.
jokes.setup: Bobby explaining why he wants to win
jokes.punchline: His admission that it's about daddy issues, not governance
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - Bobby's honesty about his daddy issues makes him sympathetic despite being wrong for the job.

jokes.text: @munchmeat2015 asks, 'Pawnee used 2 b safe. Some1 stole my car. What will u do 2 make Pawnee safe & can u help me find my car?'
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous Twitter handle and text-speak format
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of Twitter culture and social media's impact on political discourse.

jokes.text: You know, one time a guy stole some downhill skis out of my jeep. And I was so mad, I punched a mailbox. I'm against crime, and I'm not ashamed to admit it.
jokes.punchline: His random personal anecdote with ineffective response
jokes.explanation: Classic Bobby non-sequitur - the specific detail of downhill skis and punching a mailbox feels authentic to his character.

jokes.text: So Swayze runs, boom! Tackles the guy off the motorcycle... And he says, 'I used to [Bleep] guys like you in prison.'
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic but inappropriate movie recap
jokes.explanation: Pure Andy enthusiasm for inappropriate action movies - feels authentic but runs a bit long.

jokes.text: Takes the esophagus out of the neck area. You can't eat. You'll starve to death. And that is Road House.
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact description of graphic violence
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the Road House bit - Andy's casual tone describing extreme violence is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I do have an update on your time allotment, and that update is that your time is almost gone. / I have no more time left? / Well, you had some time when I started talking, but by the time I finished, your time was up.
jokes.punchline: Perd's circular non-explanation
jokes.explanation: Peak Perd Hapley - his ability to say nothing while appearing to provide information is masterfully crafted.

jokes.text: I know this. If something is dirty, we should clean it up. Let's start there. Right?
jokes.punchline: His overly simple solution delivered as profound wisdom
jokes.explanation: Good Bobby moment - treating obvious statements as breakthrough thinking.

jokes.text: You know what, I had this cleaning lady named Yolanda who was very wise. She basically raised me.
jokes.punchline: His casual admission that the help raised him
jokes.explanation: Good class commentary disguised as Bobby's oblivious storytelling - he doesn't realize what he's revealing.

jokes.text: I think we should make our parks look like my room after Yolanda finally, you know, gave in and cleaned it up for me.
jokes.punchline: His policy proposal based on domestic help dynamics
jokes.explanation: Excellent continuation - Bobby's complete lack of awareness about privilege while making policy recommendations.

jokes.text: Once again, Brandi and Leslie are essentially the same person.
jokes.punchline: Joan's completely inappropriate commentary
jokes.explanation: Good Joan unprofessionalism - drawing completely inappropriate parallels as a moderator.

jokes.text: And we all know the better-looking a park is, the more attention it will get from lady parks who want to have sex with it.
jokes.punchline: His bizarre anthropomorphization of parks
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom bizarre metaphor that somehow makes sense in his brain but sounds insane out loud.

jokes.text: Please. Vote for me to be your boyfriend.
jokes.punchline: His political metaphor for their relationship
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tom line that perfectly captures his tendency to frame everything in business/political terms.

jokes.text: When you attacked, you kind of came off like a bully, and he looked sympathetic, so, you know, y-you did a really good job of easing off.
jokes.punchline: Ben's nervous compliment that's actually criticism
jokes.explanation: Good Ben nervousness showing his struggle to manage Leslie's aggressive instincts.

jokes.text: I guess my thoughts on abortion are... You know, let's just all have a good time. I don't care about that issue. Just ask someone else.
jokes.setup: Bobby being asked about a serious political issue
jokes.punchline: His complete avoidance and childish deflection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bobby - avoiding any controversial topic with 'let's just have a good time.' The childish deflection is spot-on character work.

jokes.text: I guess my thoughts on abortion are... You know, let's just all have a good time. I don't care about that issue. Just ask someone else.
jokes.punchline: His complete avoidance and deflection to fun
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bobby response to a serious issue - treating politics like a party where tough topics should be avoided.

jokes.text: Does that guy Chris Traeger have a girlfriend, and is his penis normal?
jokes.setup: Jen asking Ben about the debate
jokes.punchline: Her extremely personal questions about Chris
jokes.explanation: Effectively uncomfortable and specific - the penis question is so random and inappropriate it's funny.

jokes.text: After I steal this cable feed, I will repay the cable company for the service we used.
jokes.punchline: His ethical approach to theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson logic - acknowledging the theft while maintaining his ethical standards.

jokes.text: Recently, my dad told me that if Leslie Knope wins the election, they'll probably have to move Sweetums to Mexico.
jokes.punchline: His casual delivery of obvious corporate blackmail
jokes.explanation: Good political satire - Bobby's oblivious delivery of what's clearly a threat makes it both funny and pointed.

jokes.text: Ah, screw it. Go get him. / Really? / Kick his ass.
jokes.setup: Ben advising Leslie to stay safe
jokes.punchline: His sudden reversal to full aggression
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Ben's protective instincts overriding his political caution.

jokes.text: How do we fix this town? I have no idea. You tell me. That's what I'm counting on. You telling me.
jokes.punchline: His admission of complete ignorance as a campaign strategy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire - Bobby's honesty about his incompetence delivered as if it's a selling point.

jokes.text: And that's how it ends. I loved it. It's called Babe. And I know it sounds corny. Talking pig. Whatever. You should all see it.
jokes.punchline: His movie recommendation as political conclusion
jokes.explanation: Peak Bobby Newport absurdity - ending a political debate by recommending a children's movie about a pig.

jokes.text: Holy [Bleep], Leslie, that was awesome. / Thanks, Bobby.
jokes.punchline: Bobby's genuine admiration for being destroyed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bobby moment - his genuine appreciation for Leslie's competence despite being politically destroyed.

jokes.text: I'll take it. And you know what else? I'm coming for you, girl...Just...Like...You...Want.
jokes.setup: Ann saying there's a chance she'll never date anyone
jokes.punchline: Tom's creepy, dramatic declaration
jokes.explanation: Effective Tom cringe but feels a bit forced - the dramatic delivery saves it.

jokes.text: Leslie Knope is scrappy like a terrier. She's smoooooth like a blended whiskey. My girl has big ideas and big feelings, and she's not afraid to express 'em.
jokes.punchline: His oddly effective metaphors and genuine enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good Tom moment where his weird metaphors actually work well for once - shows his genuine affection for Leslie.

jokes.text: Hey, party at my dad's lake house. Bring whoever you want.
jokes.punchline: His casual invitation like it's all just a game
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bobby - treating serious political defeat like the end of a fun college debate, complete with party invitation.
